meta.title: Space Wars 3000 - Complete Game Design Document
reviews_applied: Phase 4: Codex REVIEW + PRD REWRITE + Copilot SPRINT NOTE (237 comments)

features.title: Zone-Based Galaxy Layout
features.description: Galaxy split into PvE/PvP zones with dedicated Adventure Zones (5-10 sectors each). Server-side ZonePolicyService enforces security_class and reward multipliers per zone. PvP zones have increased resources and rare drops. Home sectors are on-demand, not part of shared count. 1,980 shared sectors total. Sprint scope: hardening/integration of existing implementation, not a rewrite.

prd.purpose: Purpose Establish the foundational world topology that all other systems depend on. Zones define security rules, reward multipliers, NPC behavior, and navigation constraints across the galaxy. [Implementation Status] Partially implemented — hardening/integration work, not greenfield.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Each Sector has zone_class (core, inner, outer, frontier, deep_space, adventure_pve, adventure_pvp, wormhole_node, home) enforced server-side Zone boundaries defined at universe generation; config-driven counts: Core 200, Inner 600, Outer 500, PvE Adventure 80, Frontier 400, Deep Space 100, PvP Adventure 60, Wormhole Nodes 40 Each zone carries security_class (safe/monitored/lawless), reward_multiplier (1.0x-2.5x), pvp_enabled flag, hostile_npc_level_range Single ZonePolicyService evaluates all zone rules for movement, combat, NPC spawning, loot, and map rendering Home sectors generated on-demand from user_id seed, not counted in shared total PvP Adventure Zones have 1.5-2x resource spawns, rare ore veins, and exclusive drops PvE Adventure Zones offer instanced group content (3-5 players) with periodic resets Zone config is data-driven (config/index.js), not hardcoded in service logic Sector model must carry access_mode and rule_flags columns enforced server-side to prevent zone rule duplication across movement, combat, NPC spawn, and map code [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Anti-Manipulation, Abuse Prevention, Engagement Metrics. The PRD should reconcile what already exists in the codebase with what the spec requires, identifying gaps between current implementation and target state.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes Sector.zone_class ENUM core, inner, outer, frontier, deep_space, adventure_pve, adventure_pvp, wormhole_node, home Sector.security_class ENUM safe, monitored, lawless -- derived from zone_class, overridable for events Sector.reward_multiplier FLOAT Default 1.0, set by zone config Sector.pvp_enabled BOOLEAN Derived from zone_class Sector.npc_level_min/max INTEGER Zone-driven NPC level range ZoneConfig config/index.js Sector counts, multipliers, level ranges per zone_class
prd.api_surface: API Surface GET /api/sectors/:id -- returns zone_class, security_class, pvp_enabled GET /api/zones/config -- public zone layout for map rendering (cacheable) Internal: ZonePolicyService.evaluate(sectorId, action) called by all movement/combat/trade endpoints
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Galaxy map color-coded rings: green=core, blue=inner, yellow=outer, orange=frontier, red=deep_space, purple=adventure Persistent zone legend overlay on map Entry warning modal with zone name + rules when crossing boundaries ("You are entering PvP space. Are you sure?") Zone indicator badge in HUD showing current zone + PvP status at all times Gradual visual transition between zones (color gradient on map edges)
prd.dependencies: Dependencies None -- this is Priority #1, the foundation for all other features [Codex Review] Required dependencies: none -- foundational feature [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: Sector/SectorConnection , worldPolicyService , universeGenerator
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Zone rules duplicated across services. Mitigation: Single ZonePolicyService with cached zone config; no other service checks zone rules directly. Risk: Zone boundaries feel arbitrary to players. Mitigation: Gradual difficulty transition at boundaries; narrative justification via lore. Risk: Event overrides break zone rules. Mitigation: Event zone overrides are temporary flags with TTL; expire automatically. Risk: Safe zone tactical hopping undermines risk/reward balance. Mitigation: Safe harbor departure imposes 15-minute debuff preventing jumping or trading; harbors provide repair/refuel only with no advanced commerce; prevents tactical PvP evasion. Risk: Sprint slip. Mitigation: Prioritize — if this slips, it directly slows `home-sectors`, `difficulty-scaling`, `deep-space-gates` (+14 more).
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Massive -- 2-3 weeks. Touches universe generator, Sector model, movement service, combat service, NPC spawning, loot tables, map rendering. [Codex Review] Revised complexity: Complex
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Every sector in database has a valid zone_class PvP combat blocked in safe zones, permitted in lawless zones NPC levels in each sector match zone configured range Reward multipliers apply correctly to trade, combat, and mission rewards Galaxy map renders zone colors with legend Zone transition warning fires on boundary crossing ZonePolicyService has 100% test coverage for all zone/action combinations Players receive explicit "are you sure?" confirmation modal with zone name and rules when crossing zone boundaries

features.title: Player Home Sectors (Homesteads)
features.description: Each player gets a procedurally generated home sector at galaxy rim with resource planets, NPC port, jump points to hubs, and buildable shipyards. Owner + alliance only. One-time Deluxe/Premium Starter Packs (not subscription-tied) for extra sectors. Sprint scope: hardening/integration of existing implementation, not a rewrite.

prd.purpose: Purpose Give every player a persistent, private space they own and customize. Home sectors are the emotional anchor of the game and the monetization backbone via one-time Starter Packs. [Implementation Status] Partially implemented — hardening/integration work, not greenfield.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Every player receives 1 home sector on account creation, procedurally generated from user_id seed Contents: 2-3 resource planets, 1 NPC port (basic supplies), 2-3 jump points to Core hubs, asteroid fields Owner sets access policy: Locked (owner only), Corp Only, Corp+Allies, Public Starter Packs (one-time purchase, NOT subscription): Free (1 sector/2 planets), Deluxe (2/4 + 500 gems), Premium (3/6 + shipyard + 1500 gems + cosmetic) Sectors are PERMANENT -- never removed. No subscription dependency Shipyards: cap 2 per sector, 1 per planet orbit. Requires Construction Ship + blueprint + resources Planet cap per sector: 4-6 configurable. Additional planets purchasable within cap Lazy generation: home sector created on first visit, not pre-generated 7-day full refund window on Starter Pack purchases Owned-sector ACL models must decouple permanent purchase entitlements from subscriptions; entitlement lifecycle fully auditable [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Entitlement Checks, Purchase Flow, Refund Handling, Ownership/ACL, Build/Upgrade Queue. The PRD should reconcile what already exists in the codebase with what the spec requires, identifying gaps between current implementation and target state.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes HomeSector New Model sector_id FK, owner_id FK, access_policy ENUM, seed BIGINT, created_at SectorEntitlement New Model user_id FK, pack_type ENUM(free/deluxe/premium), purchased_at, refund_deadline, status HomeSector.access_policy ENUM locked, corp_only, corp_allies, public Sector.is_home BOOLEAN Identifies home sectors in queries GuestPermission New Model sector_id FK, target_type ENUM(player/corp/alliance), permission_flags JSONB
prd.api_surface: API Surface GET /api/home-sectors -- list player owned sectors with policies PATCH /api/home-sectors/:id/access -- update access policy POST /api/store/starter-pack -- purchase pack (creates entitlement + generates sectors) POST /api/store/starter-pack/:id/refund -- refund within 7-day window GET /api/sectors/:id -- lazy-generates home sector on first access if not exists
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Home sector management panel: resource overview, visitor log, defense status, building queue, decoration placement Guest permissions: toggle switches for Dock/Trade/Harvest/Build per visitor type Starter Pack purchase: comparison table, clear pricing, refund policy displayed prominently Homestead setup wizard: pick rim location, name sector, place first buildings with guided tutorial Visiting someone else's home sector feels distinct -- their decorations, their layout, their name on the welcome screen
prd.dependencies: Dependencies sector-zones (zone_class=home), colonization (planet generation), store-philosophy (purchase flow), premium-currency (gem grants) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: sector-zones, action-audit [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: Sector/SectorConnection , worldPolicyService , universeGenerator [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `sector-zones`, `action-audit`
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Entitlement disputes on refund edge cases. Mitigation: SectorEntitlement with audit trail, 7-day refund deadline enforced server-side. Risk: Fleet/portal bypass of access control. Mitigation: Access check in movement service AND portal service, not just UI. Risk: Lazy generation causes latency spike. Mitigation: Generate via job queue async; show loading state; cache generated sectors. Risk: Economy inflation from optimized trade routes and headless-client botting. Mitigation: Dynamic trade-route margin degradation when routes are overused; account-bound (untradeable) materials for high-tier progression; behavioral bot detection via action-pattern analysis and rate limiting. Risk: Fleets, portals, and scouts bypassing owner access rules. Mitigation: Access check enforced in movement service, portal service, AND fleet service -- not just UI layer. Risk: Sprint slip. Mitigation: Prioritize — if this slips, it directly slows `offline-protection`, `store-homestead`.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Massive -- 2-3 weeks. Generator, ACL system, store integration, management UI, entitlement lifecycle. [Codex Review] Revised complexity: Complex
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria New player can enter home sector immediately after account creation Access policy blocks/permits entry correctly for all visitor types Starter Pack purchase creates correct number of sectors with correct contents Refund within 7 days removes sectors and entitlement cleanly Shipyard cap enforced: 2 per sector, 1 per orbit Home sector persists across sessions and server restarts

features.title: Universe Scaling Config
features.description: Start with 1,980 shared sectors, scalable to 3,500 without re-gen. Home sectors are on-demand. Requires PostgreSQL, Redis, background workers, and tick-budget telemetry before 1k CCU. Budget ~$60-100/mo to start.

prd.purpose: Purpose Define infrastructure scaling roadmap from 200 concurrent to 3,000+ without requiring a rewrite. Provides cost projections and performance budgets at each tier. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Four scaling tiers: Launch (1,980 sectors/200 concurrent/$40-60/mo), Growth (2,000/500/$130-175), Scale (3,500/1,000/$400-600), Future (10,000/3,000+/$1,500-2,500) Sector count increase is config-only; universe generator seeds deterministically Per-sector tick budget: max 5ms at 10 ticks/second with monitoring alerts Per-query budget: max 50ms for sector load, 10ms for movement validation PostgreSQL migration path from SQLite with zero-downtime cutover plan Redis cache layer for hot sector data, NPC state, player positions Background workers split from request handlers at Growth tier Oversell ratio 7:1 (not all sectors active simultaneously) Mandatory infrastructure prerequisites before 1k concurrent: PostgreSQL, Redis/cache layer, background workers, and tick-budget telemetry Measurable per-tick budgets: sector generation <50ms, query budget per tick, state mutation budget per player [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Migration/Backfill, Acceptance Criteria, Error States. The PRD should define concrete state machines, data contracts, and error handling before implementation begins.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes ScalingConfig config/index.js Tier definitions with sector counts, budgets, infrastructure specs PerformanceBudget Monitoring sector_tick_ms, query_latency_ms, cache_hit_rate alerts Migration scripts server/migrations/ SQLite-to-PostgreSQL data migration + verification
prd.api_surface: API Surface GET /api/admin/metrics -- sector tick times, query latencies, cache hit rates, player distribution GET /api/admin/scaling -- current tier status and utilization No player-facing API changes; scaling is transparent
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Players experience no visible difference between tiers Universe feels alive at low population via NPC caravans, environmental events, dynamic port activity Admin dashboard: real-time tick budgets, query performance, player distribution heat map Universe must feel alive at low density (~0.4 players/sector average): NPC trade activity, environmental events, dynamic port activity fill gaps Frontend: lazy-load sector data, cache aggressively on map, WebSocket updates for nearby sectors only
prd.dependencies: Dependencies job-queue (background workers), live-ops-observability (metrics/alerts) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: job-queue, live-ops-observability, sector-instance [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: tickService , socketService , realtimeCombatService [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `job-queue`, `live-ops-observability`, `sector-instance`
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: SQLite-to-PostgreSQL migration breaks queries. Mitigation: Sequelize abstracts dialect; full test suite runs against both DBs. Risk: Universe feels empty at low player density. Mitigation: NPC trade caravans, random events, port activity fill gaps. Risk: Cost estimates are optimistic. Mitigation: Load test at each tier BEFORE committing to player counts. Risk: Economy inflation from optimized trade routes and headless-client botting. Mitigation: Dynamic trade-route margin degradation when routes are overused; account-bound (untradeable) materials for high-tier progression; behavioral bot detection via action-pattern analysis and rate limiting.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Medium per tier -- Launch is current state. Each subsequent tier is 1-2 weeks of infrastructure work. [Codex Review] Revised complexity: Complex
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Sector tick budget stays under 5ms per sector under load test at target concurrent Universe generator produces identical layouts for same seed across all config changes PostgreSQL migration completes with zero data loss and passing test suite Redis cache reduces DB queries per tick by 60%+ at Growth tier

features.title: Zone Difficulty Scaling
features.description: NPC levels and hazard intensity scale with distance from Core. Purchasable private Adventure Sectors (PvE, difficulty 1-10). AI-powered NPCs with bounded memory (5 recent + summary), LLM fallback to templates.

prd.purpose: Purpose Progressive challenge scaling from Core to Deep Space, plus AI-driven NPC interactions that create emotional connections and unpredictable encounters. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements NPC levels scale with zone: Core L1-3, Inner L3-6, Outer L5-8, Frontier L7-10, Deep Space L9-10+ Reward multipliers: Core 1.0x, Inner 1.3x, Outer 1.6x, Frontier 2.0x, Deep Space 2.5x Purchasable private Adventure Sectors (PvE, difficulty 1-10) LLM-driven dialogue for: flagship crew, boss NPCs, named quest-givers, mercenaries ONLY NPC Memory: per-NPC-per-player log, max 5 recent interactions + 1 summary (500 token cap) Fallback: templated personality responses within 500ms when LLM unavailable Adaptive boss tactics: analyze player fleet, change patterns mid-fight Dynamic quest generation from galaxy state (shortages, conflicts, raider activity) NPC memory: bounded NpcMemory table with composite index (npc_id, player_id), max 5 recent interactions in full detail + summarized history string (500 tokens max). Summarization/versioning with strict token budgets LLM fallback: if AI provider unavailable, use templated responses based on NPC personality type. Never let AI call fail visibly to player [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Entitlement Checks, Purchase Flow, Refund Handling. The PRD should define concrete state machines, data contracts, and error handling before implementation begins.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes NpcMemory New Model npc_id FK, player_id FK, interactions JSONB (max 5), summary TEXT (500 tokens), updated_at NpcMemory index Composite (npc_id, player_id) unique NpcPersona New Model npc_id FK, persona_template TEXT, personality_traits JSONB, is_ai_enabled BOOLEAN DifficultyConfig config/index.js Level ranges, hazard scales, reward multipliers per zone
prd.api_surface: API Surface POST /api/npc/:id/interact -- triggers dialogue with memory context, returns response GET /api/npc/:id/memory -- player interaction history (admin/debug only) POST /api/adventure-sectors/purchase -- buy private adventure sector at chosen difficulty
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification NPC memory surfaces organically in dialogue -- no memory UI exposed to players Boss NPCs telegraph adaptive tactics visually (pattern changes, reinforcement calls) Zone difficulty gradient visible on galaxy map via color and tooltip LLM failures are invisible -- fallback templates match NPC personality NPC memory surfaces organically in dialogue ("Ah, you are back. Last time you traded Xenon Crystals here...") -- no explicit memory UI needed
prd.dependencies: Dependencies sector-zones (zone_class drives difficulty), job-queue (async LLM calls), action-audit (interaction logging) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: sector-zones, clan-spawning, pvp-rewards [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: Sector/SectorConnection , worldPolicyService , universeGenerator [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `sector-zones`, `clan-spawning`, `pvp-rewards`
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: LLM API costs spiral. Mitigation: Strict 500-token budgets, cache common interactions, selective AI for named NPCs only. Risk: Memory grows unbounded. Mitigation: Bounded at 5 recent + summary per pair; weekly compaction job. Risk: LLM latency ruins UX. Mitigation: Async call with 500ms timeout; fallback template response. Risk: Database bloat and state-sync bottlenecks at peak CCU. Mitigation: Hard pagination on all list queries; cold storage archival for stale entities (>90 days inactive); strict rate-limiting on state mutations per tick per player.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Complex -- 2 weeks. Zone difficulty config is simple; NPC memory + LLM integration is the bulk.
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria NPC levels in each zone match configured ranges Named NPCs remember prior interactions with same player across sessions LLM fallback activates within 500ms when API unavailable NpcMemory stays under 4KB per pair with automatic compaction Boss NPCs visibly adapt tactics based on player fleet composition

features.title: Portals + Static Wormholes
features.description: Two systems: (1) Player Portals -- purchasable, owned-sector-only, with destination picker modal. (2) Static Wormholes -- public infrastructure in un-ownable sectors, one hop from hyperlanes. 15-20 pairs. Sprint scope: hardening/integration of existing implementation, not a rewrite.
workflow: Static wormhole pairs in un-ownable sectors

prd.purpose: Purpose Provide fast long-range travel infrastructure: player-owned portals for private use and public static wormholes as shared highway system. [Implementation Status] Partially implemented — hardening/integration work, not greenfield.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Player Portals: purchasable (gems/credits) or craftable from rare components (Warp Crystal, Void Conduit, Spatial Stabilizer) Portals placeable ONLY in player-owned sectors. Max 1 per sector Portal use: click to open destination picker (all owned sectors), instant travel, no cooldown Static Wormholes: 15-20 pairs placed at universe generation in un-ownable sectors Static wormholes always 1 hop from a hyperlane. Free, public, indestructible Connect major zones: Home rim to Core, Core to Outer, Outer to Frontier entries Anti-camp: 5-second arrival invulnerability at wormhole exits Route planner includes wormhole connections in pathfinding graph Static wormholes modeled as first-class SectorConnection entities (not special map art) with route-planner weighting and public-use enforcement Anti-camp entry protection: 5-second arrival invulnerability with visual shield effect [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Entitlement Checks, Purchase Flow, Refund Handling, Ownership/ACL, Construction State. The PRD should reconcile what already exists in the codebase with what the spec requires, identifying gaps between current implementation and target state.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes Portal New Model id, sector_id FK, owner_id FK, type ENUM(player/static), paired_portal_id FK, created_at PortalDestination New Model portal_id FK, destination_sector_id FK (for player portals with multi-dest) Sector.has_portal BOOLEAN Quick lookup flag SectorConnection type ENUM Add portal_static and portal_player to existing hyperlane type
prd.api_surface: API Surface POST /api/portals -- place portal in owned sector POST /api/portals/:id/travel -- use portal to travel (validates ownership/access) GET /api/portals -- list player portals and nearby static wormholes DELETE /api/portals/:id -- remove player portal
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Map rendering: purple animated lines for static wormholes, blue pulsing for player portals Click portal: destination picker modal listing owned sectors with distance/contents Transit animation (brief warp effect) to sell the experience Arrival invulnerability: 5-second shield glow effect at destination Rare portal components shown in crafting UI with drop location hints Distinctive portal visual treatment on galaxy map: animated rings or pulsing nodes, clearly different from normal hyperlane connections Transit animation: brief loading/warp effect when using portal to sell the experience
prd.dependencies: Dependencies sector-zones (un-ownable sector designation), home-sectors (owned sector validation), route-planner (pathfinding integration) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: deep-space-gates, protected-lanes, route-planner, action-audit [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: worldPolicyService , sectorGraphService , shipService/fleetService [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `deep-space-gates`, `protected-lanes`, `route-planner` (+1 more)
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Portals bypass zone security policies. Mitigation: Portal travel validates destination zone rules same as normal movement. Risk: Anti-camp timer exploited. Mitigation: Invulnerability breaks on any offensive action; 5s is enough to orient, not enough to attack. Risk: Portal component farming becomes grindy. Mitigation: Balance drop rates; always available in store as alternative.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Medium -- 1-2 weeks. Portal model, travel service, map rendering, route planner integration. [Codex Review] Revised complexity: Complex
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Player can place portal in owned sector and travel to other owned sectors instantly Static wormholes appear on map and are usable by any player Route planner includes wormhole connections in shortest-path calculation Anti-camp invulnerability activates on arrival and breaks on offensive action Portal placement blocked in non-owned sectors

features.title: Unstable Wormholes (Adventure Access)
features.description: Temporary wormholes to adventure clusters lasting DAYS. Auto-teleport home on close (non-bound cargo dropped as salvage at origin to prevent smuggling). 5-10 active at once. Admins can launch event wormholes on the fly.

prd.purpose: Purpose Create time-limited exploration events that drive FOMO, social content, and multi-day expedition gameplay through temporary wormholes to adventure clusters. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Unstable wormholes spawn periodically connecting shared galaxy to adventure clusters Duration: DAYS (not hours) for multi-day expedition gameplay 5-10 active at any time across galaxy Server-wide announcement on spawn with location, countdown, and destination preview Auto-teleport players to home sector when wormhole closes. Non-bound cargo and unequipped loot dropped as salvage at origin point to prevent risk-free smuggling Wormhole state persisted in database (survives server restart) Admin panel: create custom event wormholes with duration, destination, difficulty, rewards Occupancy rules prevent overcrowding inside adventure clusters Persisted wormhole state with scheduler: occupancy rules, expiry recall logic, and event announcements handled outside the request path. Do not implement closure/teleport behavior in memory only [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Ownership/ACL, Construction State, Toll Rules, Discovery/Visibility. The PRD should define concrete state machines, data contracts, and error handling before implementation begins.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes UnstableWormhole New Model id, entry_sector_id FK, dest_cluster_id, spawn_time, expiry_time, status ENUM(active/closing/closed), created_by WormholeOccupant New Model wormhole_id FK, player_id FK, entered_at ScheduledWormhole New Model template config for admin-created event wormholes
prd.api_surface: API Surface GET /api/wormholes/unstable -- list active unstable wormholes with countdowns POST /api/wormholes/:id/enter -- enter unstable wormhole (validates capacity) POST /api/admin/wormholes -- create event wormhole (admin only) Cron/Job: WormholeExpiryJob checks and closes expired wormholes, teleports occupants
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Flickering animated portal icon on galaxy map with countdown timer Server-wide toast/banner: "Unstable wormhole detected in Sector 47! Stability: 2 days 4 hours" Dramatic warp effect on entry; dramatic collapse animation if caught inside at close Admin event panel: wizard for creating event wormholes (duration, rewards, announcement text) Server-wide announcement on appearance ("Unstable wormhole detected in Sector 47! Estimated stability: 3 days") Galaxy map: flickering unstable portal icon with countdown timer. Collapse event triggers dramatic animation and home-sector recall (non-bound cargo and unequipped loot dropped as salvage at origin point)
prd.dependencies: Dependencies sector-zones (adventure cluster sectors), job-queue (expiry processing), socket (live announcements) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: adventure-sectors, live-ops-observability, notifications [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: worldPolicyService , sectorGraphService , shipService/fleetService [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `adventure-sectors`, `live-ops-observability`, `notifications`
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Server restart during wormhole active. Mitigation: Full state persisted in DB; startup job resumes all active wormholes. Risk: Players exploit close-teleport for free travel. Mitigation: Teleport always goes to home sector, not arbitrary destination. Risk: Overcrowding in adventure clusters. Mitigation: Occupancy cap per wormhole; queue if full. Risk: Database bloat and state-sync bottlenecks at peak CCU. Mitigation: Hard pagination on all list queries; cold storage archival for stale entities (>90 days inactive); strict rate-limiting on state mutations per tick per player.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Complex -- 1-2 weeks. Scheduler, persistence, teleport logic, admin tools, map rendering.
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Wormholes spawn on schedule and appear on galaxy map with countdown Players can enter and are placed in adventure cluster Expiry auto-teleports all occupants to home sector Wormhole state survives server restart Admin can create event wormholes via panel

features.title: Jump Drive Component
features.description: High-tier ship component: 3-5 sector jumps at once, max 60-second cooldown, Tier 4-5. Endgame long-range mobility.

prd.purpose: Purpose Give endgame ships independent long-range mobility without relying on wormholes or gates, creating tactical depth in PvP and strategic movement options. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Jump Drive is a high-tier ship component (Tier 4-5) Range: 3-5 sectors per jump (tier-dependent) Cooldown: max 60 seconds between jumps Fuel cost: 3-5x normal travel fuel per sector skipped Cannot jump into protected/locked/home sectors without permission Jump leaves a "jump signature" visible on scanners for 30 seconds (PvP counter-play) Server-authoritative: destination legality, cooldown, fuel validated in one service call Zone policy checks reused from normal movement (not duplicated) Server-authoritative validation: destination legality, cooldown, fuel burn, and jump signatures all verified in one service Reuse ZonePolicyService for destination checks -- do not duplicate world-policy logic [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Migration/Backfill, Acceptance Criteria, Error States. The PRD should define concrete state machines, data contracts, and error handling before implementation begins.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes Component type ENUM Add jump_drive to existing component types JumpDriveStats Component config range, cooldown_sec, fuel_multiplier per tier JumpSignature Transient ship_id, destination_sector_id, visible_until timestamp (30s TTL)
prd.api_surface: API Surface POST /api/ships/:id/jump -- initiate jump (validates cooldown, fuel, destination legality) GET /api/sectors/:id/signatures -- visible jump signatures in sector (for PvP tracking)
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Charge-up animation: 3-5 second warmup with engine glow building intensity Cooldown timer visible on ship HUD (not hidden in component panel) Jump signature: brief "warp trail" visible to nearby players showing destination direction Range tier indicator in component tooltip showing jump radius on mini-map Satisfying charge-up animation (3-5 second warmup with engine glow building). Jump signature reveals destination to nearby players (PvP tension mechanic) Cooldown visible on ship HUD at all times, not hidden in component panel
prd.dependencies: Dependencies components (component system), sector-zones (zone policy validation) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: route-planner, action-audit, econ-sinks [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: movementService , shipService , sectorGraphService [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `route-planner`, `action-audit`, `econ-sinks`
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Chain-jumping bypasses protected routes. Mitigation: Jump validates same zone policies as normal movement. Risk: Jump signature reveals too much in PvP. Mitigation: 30-second TTL; only shows direction, not exact destination sector.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Medium -- 3-5 days. New component type, jump service, signature system, UX. [Codex Review] Revised complexity: Complex
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Jump drive moves ship 3-5 sectors in one action Cooldown prevents immediate re-jump Fuel consumed at 3-5x rate Jump blocked into locked/protected sectors Signature visible to other players for 30 seconds

features.title: Player-Built Gates + Commercial Portals
features.description: Three tiers: Personal gates (home-to-owned, instant+cooldown), Corporation gates (tolls, PvP-destructible), Commercial PvE-to-PvP portals with 10 Space Hub types (Casino, Arena, Paint Dock, Racing Circuit, Intelligence Broker, etc.).

prd.purpose: Purpose Enable player-built fast-travel infrastructure with three trust tiers (personal, corporate, commercial), creating player economy through toll gates and Space Hub destinations. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Tier 1 Personal Gates: built in owned sectors with Construction Ship. Instant travel from home with cooldown Tier 2 Corporation Gates: expensive structures in Outer/Frontier. Permanent 2-sector link. Corp sets toll price. PvP-destructible Tier 3 Commercial Portals: PvE-to-PvP bridges. Robust warning modal. PvP sector owners cannot attack PvE visitors unless attacked first (15% stat penalty + 1h ban for aggressor) 10 Space Hub types buildable by PvP sector owners: Casino, Paint Dock, Exotic Market, Arena, Bounty Board, Recruitment Office, Black Market, Racing Circuit, Salvage Yard, Intelligence Broker Gate construction requires Construction Ship + rare materials Toll system: corp sets price, auto-collected on use, revenue split to corp treasury First-aggressor tracking for commercial portal protection Portal owner advertising modal popup for visitors Requires Gate, PortalDestination, TollLedger, and first-aggressor audit models. features.title: Anti-Griefing Design
features.description: 6-8 wide entry corridors into PvP, 30-second invuln buffer on entry. Tourist Pass (20 min immunity, in-game currency or store). Tourists cannot enter owned sectors. Anti-scouting restrictions.

prd.purpose: Purpose Prevent griefing from killing PvP participation. Every protection mechanism must be discoverable by new players without reading the wiki. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements 6-8 wide entry corridors into PvP (not chokepoints) 30-second invulnerability buffer on PvP zone entry Tourist Pass: 20 minutes immunity, purchasable with credits or store. Cannot enter owned sectors. Eligibility: no PvP sector ownership, no recent PvP combat, account age 7+ days, level 3+ Bounty escalation: repeated kills of same low-level player auto-generates escalating bounty Newbie Shield: players under L5 have "New Pilot" tag; killing them yields 0 rewards + "Bully" debuff Revenge flag: 10-minute 15% damage bonus against your killer Safe logout within 30 seconds when not in active combat Distress beacon: broadcasts location to allied/neutral players All protections backed by persisted state models (not ad-hoc timers) Each protection mechanism persisted as first-class backend object: TouristPass, ProtectionState, BountyCase, CrimePenalty, InsurancePolicy, EmergencyWarpRequest -- evaluated by CombatPolicyService and TravelPolicyService, not UI-driven timers Tourist Pass eligibility requires no-territory, no-recent-PvP, and new-player-cohort checks to prevent scouting abuse (no arbitrary account-age gate that blocks genuine first-time users) [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Anti-Manipulation, Abuse Prevention, Engagement Metrics, Entitlement Checks, Purchase Flow. The PRD should define concrete state machines, data contracts, and error handling before implementation begins.

features.title: Store Design Philosophy
features.description: Everything purchasable MUST be grindable. Formal entitlement taxonomy (permanent, consumable, cosmetic, convenience) with hard boundaries. One-time Starter Packs for sectors (not subscription-tied). Premium subscription for convenience only. Free players on the SAME map.

prd.purpose: Purpose Establish the monetization framework as an entitlement taxonomy with hard rules preventing pay-to-win, ensuring player trust and sustainable revenue. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.

Later store features must not leak power or create refund edge cases "Angry Reddit post" test: every purchasable item must also be earnable in-game. No loot boxes or randomized purchases. 24h refund window on all purchases (7-day exception for Homestead permanent entitlements) [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Entitlement Checks, Purchase Flow, Refund Handling. The PRD should define concrete state machines, data contracts, and error handling before implementation begins.

features.title: Turn-Based Space Combat
features.description: Shields, armor, crits (10%, 2x dmg), flee mechanics. Max 50 rounds. 2% component degradation per round. 70% loot chance. Sprint scope: hardening/integration of existing implementation, not a rewrite.

prd.purpose: Purpose Provide the core combat system with deterministic, auditable, and idempotent resolution for both PvE and PvP encounters. [Implementation Status] Partially implemented — hardening/integration work, not greenfield.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Shields absorb damage first, then armor, then hull Critical hits: 10% chance, 2x damage Flee mechanics: success based on engine tier vs opponent speed Max 50 rounds per fight Component degradation: 2% per round Loot: 70% chance, quality scales with NPC level Deterministic combat log: every round recorded for replay Idempotent reward grants (no double XP/loot on retry) Combat results journaled in action-audit Auditability: deterministic combat logs and reward journaling. Keep combat results idempotent so retries or reconnects never double-pay loot or XP [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Idempotency, Replay Prevention, Balance Tuning. The PRD should reconcile what already exists in the codebase with what the spec requires, identifying gaps between current implementation and target state.
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes CombatLog Existing Model Ensure deterministic round-by-round data stored CombatReward New Model combat_id FK, player_id FK, reward_type, amount, idempotency_key UNIQUE, claimed BOOLEAN
prd.api_surface: API Surface POST /api/combat/attack -- initiate combat POST /api/combat/:id/action -- submit round action (attack/flee/special) GET /api/combat/:id/log -- deterministic combat log for replay POST /api/combat/:id/claim -- claim rewards (idempotent)
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Combat replay viewer: step through each round Damage breakdown per round: shields/armor/hull "What if" simulator: test loadout against known enemies Auto-combat for trivial fights (NPC 10+ levels below) Post-combat tips: "You could have fled at round 3" Combat replay viewer (step through each turn). Damage breakdown per turn. "What if" simulator against known enemies. Auto-combat for trivial fights (NPC 10+ levels below). Post-combat tips
prd.dependencies: Dependencies components (stat calculation), action-audit (combat logging) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: npc-types, ship-designer [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: combatService , CombatLog , shipService [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `npc-types`, `ship-designer`
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Double reward on reconnect. Mitigation: Idempotent claims with unique idempotency_key per combat+player. Risk: Non-deterministic replay. Mitigation: Seeded RNG; combat log stores seed + all inputs. Risk: Unchecked griefing and player churn via alt-account abuse of first-aggressor flags. Mitigation: Diminishing returns on PvP loot from same target per 24h (100%→50%→10%→0%); hardware ID tracking for alt-account detection; escalating NPC security response to persistent zone camping. Risk: Sprint slip. Mitigation: Prioritize — if this slips, it directly slows `realtime-combat`, `ground-combat`.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Medium -- existing system needs audit/replay additions. 1 week. [Codex Review] Revised complexity: Complex
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Combat resolves deterministically with logged rounds Rewards claimed exactly once per combat per player Combat replay reconstructs fight accurately Auto-combat works for trivial encounters

features.title: AI Agent Companion
features.description: Player-linked AI companion runs as a scoped sub-account with its own ship/crew, audited action budget, queue-backed directives, and API permissions limited to assigned assets. Trade/defense/scouting ship first; governor and marketplace extensions later.
workflow: Agent authentication system (agent account linked to player)
Agent API access (same endpoints as players)
Agent permission system (what it can/cannot do)

prd.purpose: Purpose The most ambitious feature: AI companion that plays the game as a real participant using the same API, with scoped permissions, crew management, governor mode, warlord NPCs, and semantic memory. [Implementation Status] Net-new system — requires full implementation.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements 1 agent per player, linked as AgentAccount sub-account Agent authenticates via JWT with agent role (ABAC-style permissions) Ship-scoped auth: only assigned ship, assigned crew, approved colonies/routes Agent crew: own ship crew with same skill/gear/Identity Disk system Directives enqueue jobs via scheduler with rate limits (500ms between actions), spend caps, concurrency caps, kill switch Kill switch: immediate revocation flag + cancel queued jobs Agent actions logged to action-audit ledger Agent spectate mode: read-only real-time view via normal socket streams 4-phase rollout: trade routes -> colony management -> combat -> LLM-driven autonomous play AI Warlord NPCs: server-controlled agents with procedural personas, never same personality twice Agent marketplace: signed configuration manifests only (no code/credentials) Governor mode: same agent principal with narrower sector-scoped permissions File storage: PostgreSQL JSONB for state, pgvector for semantic memory, blob storage for strategy exports ABAC-style authorization: agent may only access assigned ship, assigned crew, owner-approved colonies/routes, and explicitly whitelisted action families STOP AGENT sets immediate revocation flag on principal AND cancels all queued jobs by agent ID -- not merely stops issuing new directives Spectate mode reads from normal ship telemetry/socket streams with observer scope, not a hidden backdoor API Agent marketplace sells/shares signed configuration manifests and policy bundles, never executable code or raw credentials. Agent-to-agent interaction only through normal game actions/messages Governor mode: same agent principal with narrower permission set over one governed sector plus periodic report jobs, not a separate hidden superuser path [Codex PRD Guidance] Add sections for Rate Limiting, Error Recovery, Resource Budgets. The PRD should define concrete state machines, data contracts, and error handling before implementation begins.

prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Mission Control panel: agent ship on mini-map, directives sidebar, budget gauges, activity feed Kill switch: big red STOP AGENT button Spectate mode (mobile priority): real-time read-only view Directive editor: drag to reorder priority Warlord encounters: unique named NPCs with personality-driven dialogue Agent marketplace: browse/buy/sell strategy manifests with ratings "Mission control" panel: agent ship on mini-map with current route, directive list (drag to reorder), budget gauges, scrolling activity feed. Kill switch: big red STOP button for immediate halt. Spectate mode (mobile priority): real-time read-only view of agent actions
prd.dependencies: Dependencies job-queue (directive execution), action-audit (all agent actions), automation (shared pipeline), crew-species (agent crew), sector-zones (governor permissions) [Codex Review] Required dependencies: automation, action-audit, job-queue [Sprint Hotspots] Key files: AI providers , agentService , socketService [Sprint Sequencing] Must schedule after: `automation`, `action-audit`, `job-queue`
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Agent exploits game bug. Mitigation: Player responsible; all actions audited; kill switch available. Risk: Agent loop overloads server. Mitigation: Rate limit 500ms; concurrency cap; spend cap; job queue throttling. Risk: Warlord memory cost creep. Mitigation: Bounded vector recall; tiered memory (named rivals only); weekly compaction. Risk: Marketplace sells malicious configs. Mitigation: Signed manifests only; no executable code; admin review for flagged listings. Risk: Economy inflation from optimized trade routes and headless-client botting. Mitigation: Dynamic trade-route margin degradation when routes are overused; account-bound (untradeable) materials for high-tier progression; behavioral bot detection via action-pattern analysis and rate limiting. Risk: Unbounded vector recall cost for warlord NPC personas. Mitigation: Bounded and tiered semantic memory; named rivals get detailed recall, but generic warlord/player interactions use summarized context only. Risk: Agent loop directly hitting live HTTP requests causes rate/cost issues. Mitigation: Directives enqueue into scheduler/worker layer with per-agent rate limits, spend caps, concurrency caps, and kill switches.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Massive -- 6+ weeks total across 4 phases. Phase 1 (trade) is 2 weeks. Each subsequent phase adds 1-2 weeks. [Codex Review] Revised complexity: Complex
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Agent authenticates and performs actions within permitted scope Kill switch immediately halts all agent activity Spectate mode shows real-time agent actions Agent crew managed correctly with skills/gear Warlord NPCs have unique procedural personas Agent marketplace listings are signed and validated

features.title: Unified Anti-Cheat & Anti-Exploit Architecture
features.description: Centralized anti-cheat infrastructure consolidating device fingerprinting, alt-account detection, rate limiting, bot detection, trade-route abuse prevention, account-bound enforcement, combat idempotency, and reward journaling into one authoritative service layer.

prd.purpose: Purpose Consolidate scattered anti-cheat provisions from 56+ features into a single, authoritative anti-cheat service layer. Provides device fingerprinting (from pvp-rewards), trade-route abuse detection (from dynamic-pricing), bot detection, rate limiting, and reward integrity guarantees that all game systems can depend on.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements DeviceFingerprintService: collect hardware ID, browser fingerprint, IP correlation; link to user accounts; flag multi-account clusters AltAccountDetector: behavioral signal analysis (play-time overlap, trade patterns, identical configs), IP correlation, hardware ID matching; confidence scoring with human review queue RateLimitService: centralized, configurable per-endpoint and per-tick rate limits; token bucket algorithm with Redis backing; graduated response (warn → throttle → temp-ban) BotDetectionEngine: action-pattern analysis (click timing regularity, route optimization patterns), headless-client fingerprinting, CAPTCHA challenge triggers TradeRouteAbuseDetector: margin degradation engine tracking per-player route profitability; diminishing returns on repeated identical routes; consolidates RouteUsageTracker from dynamic-pricing Account-bound material enforcement: prevent cross-account transfers of bound items; server-side validation on all trade/transfer endpoints CombatIdempotencyService: replay verification for combat results; detect duplicate combat submissions; cryptographic action signing RewardJournalService: idempotency keys on all reward grants; append-only audit log; reconciliation jobs to detect anomalies All detection events feed into action-audit for GM review Configurable sensitivity thresholds via GameSetting (not hardcoded)
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes DeviceFingerprint New Model user_id FK, hardware_hash, browser_hash, ip_history JSONB, first_seen, last_seen, confidence_score BehavioralSignal New Model user_id FK, signal_type ENUM, raw_data JSONB, score FLOAT, detected_at, resolved BOOLEAN RateLimitPolicy New Model endpoint_key, bucket_size INT, refill_rate FLOAT, penalty_escalation JSONB BotDetectionEvent New Model user_id FK, detection_type ENUM, evidence JSONB, action_taken ENUM, created_at RewardJournal New Model idempotency_key UNIQUE, user_id FK, reward_type, quantity, source_action, granted_at RouteUsageTracker New Model user_id FK, route_hash, usage_count INT, last_used, margin_multiplier FLOAT
prd.api_surface: API Surface Internal: AntiCheatService.validateAction(userId, actionType, context) — called by all game endpoints Internal: RateLimitService.check(userId, endpoint) — middleware integration Internal: RewardJournalService.grant(userId, rewardType, qty, idempotencyKey) GET /api/admin/anti-cheat/alerts — GM dashboard feed POST /api/admin/anti-cheat/resolve/:alertId — mark alert reviewed GET /api/admin/anti-cheat/player/:userId — full anti-cheat profile for a player
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification No player-facing UI — all detection is server-side and invisible GM admin panel: alerts list with severity, player profile with fingerprint history, bulk action tools Rate limit responses return standard 429 with Retry-After header Bot detection CAPTCHA challenge: minimal modal, does not interrupt legitimate play
prd.dependencies: Dependencies action-audit (event ingestion for all detection signals) job-queue (async detection pipelines, reconciliation jobs) live-ops-observability (metrics on detection rates, false positives) pvp-rewards (consolidates DeviceFingerprint model) dynamic-pricing (consolidates RouteUsageTracker)
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: False positives ban legitimate players. Mitigation: All automated actions are soft (throttle/flag) — only GM-reviewed actions result in bans; confidence scoring with configurable thresholds. Risk: Fingerprinting blocked by privacy tools. Mitigation: Multiple signal layers (hardware, behavioral, IP); no single signal is decisive; graceful degradation. Risk: Performance overhead on hot paths. Mitigation: Rate limit checks are O(1) Redis lookups; behavioral analysis runs async via job queue; combat validation is lightweight hash comparison. Risk: Regulatory/privacy concerns with fingerprinting. Mitigation: Fingerprints are hashed, not raw data; retention policy with auto-expiry; compliant with ToS disclosure.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Massive — 3-4 weeks. Touches every game endpoint (rate limiting middleware), combat service, reward grants, trading, and requires new detection infrastructure.
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria All reward grants go through RewardJournalService with idempotency keys Rate limiting active on all API endpoints with configurable per-endpoint policies Device fingerprinting captures hardware + browser + IP signals on login Alt-account clusters detected with >80% precision in test scenarios Bot detection flags automated play patterns within 30 minutes of detection Trade route margin degradation activates after configurable repeat threshold GM dashboard shows all alerts with one-click investigation Zero false-positive bans — all automated responses are soft (throttle/warn only) All detection events appear in action-audit log

features.title: GM/Moderation Tooling
features.description: Comprehensive GM and moderation dashboard providing player reporting, appeal/dispute workflows, penalty escalation, chat moderation, live spectate, bulk ban tools, and full audit trail integration.

prd.purpose: Purpose Provide game masters with a unified toolset for handling player reports, investigating violations, issuing penalties, moderating chat, and managing appeals. Consolidates moderation needs scattered across pvp-choke (appeal workflow), messaging (chat moderation), and community-events (player behavior) into one system.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Player Report System: in-game report button on player profiles, death screens, and chat; report categories: griefing, botting, exploiting, harassment, RMT; evidence attachment: combat log links, chat log references, screenshots Appeal/Dispute Workflow: player submits appeal from account settings; GM reviews evidence, player history, anti-cheat signals; resolution options: dismiss, reduce penalty, overturn; notification to both reporter and reported player GM Dashboard: search players by name/ID/IP, view full audit log, account history, anti-cheat profile; issue penalties directly; grant refunds or compensation; view active reports queue with priority scoring Chat Moderation: configurable profanity filter with word lists and regex patterns; message flagging for GM review; player block lists (per-player and global); mute functionality with duration Penalty Escalation: warning → mute (1h) → mute (24h) → temp ban (3d) → temp ban (7d) → perma ban; automatic escalation based on violation history; manual override for severity Live Spectate Mode: GM can observe any player's session in real-time without their knowledge; read-only view of player actions, position, inventory; used for investigating active reports Bulk Action Tools: select multiple players for batch penalties (ban waves); import/export ban lists; scheduled penalty execution All moderation actions logged to action-audit with GM identity and justification
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes PlayerReport New Model reporter_id FK, reported_id FK, category ENUM, description TEXT, evidence JSONB, status ENUM(open/investigating/resolved/dismissed), priority INT, created_at AppealCase New Model user_id FK, penalty_id FK, appeal_text TEXT, gm_response TEXT, status ENUM(pending/reviewing/resolved), resolution ENUM, created_at, resolved_at ModerationAction New Model gm_id FK, target_id FK, action_type ENUM, severity INT, justification TEXT, expires_at, created_at ChatFilter New Model pattern TEXT, filter_type ENUM(word/regex/phrase), action ENUM(block/flag/replace), active BOOLEAN BanRecord New Model user_id FK, ban_type ENUM(mute/temp_ban/perma_ban), reason TEXT, issued_by FK, duration INT, expires_at, active BOOLEAN
prd.api_surface: API Surface POST /api/reports — submit player report (authenticated) GET /api/reports/mine — player's submitted reports and their status POST /api/appeals — submit appeal (authenticated) GET /api/admin/moderation/reports — GM report queue with filters POST /api/admin/moderation/action — issue penalty PUT /api/admin/moderation/appeal/:id — resolve appeal GET /api/admin/moderation/player/:id — full player moderation profile POST /api/admin/moderation/bulk-action — batch penalties GET /api/admin/moderation/spectate/:userId — WebSocket spectate stream CRUD /api/admin/moderation/chat-filters — manage chat filter rules
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Player-facing: report button (flag icon) on player profiles and post-death screen; appeal form in account settings; notification bell for report resolution GM dashboard: dedicated /admin/moderation route; report queue with priority badges; player search with autocomplete; one-click penalty shortcuts; evidence viewer with combat log replay Chat moderation: real-time filter preview; filter test tool; flagged message review queue
prd.dependencies: Dependencies action-audit (all moderation actions logged) anti-cheat-system (anti-cheat signals feed into GM investigation view) messaging (chat moderation integration) socket (live spectate WebSocket stream) notifications (report resolution notifications)
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: GM abuse of power. Mitigation: All GM actions audited with justification; senior GM review required for perma bans; action rate alerts for unusual GM activity. Risk: Report spam/abuse. Mitigation: Rate limit on reports per player; report credibility scoring based on history; false report penalties. Risk: Spectate mode privacy concerns. Mitigation: Spectate access restricted to senior GMs; all spectate sessions logged; auto-timeout after 30 minutes. Risk: Chat filter circumvention. Mitigation: Regex + phonetic matching + Levenshtein distance; community-reported additions; ML-based escalation for repeated offenders.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Massive — 3 weeks. Full moderation workflow, GM dashboard, chat integration, spectate mode, and penalty system.
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Players can report others from profile and death screen with category and evidence GM dashboard shows prioritized report queue with investigation tools Penalty escalation automatically applies correct tier based on history Appeals workflow: submit → review → resolve with notifications to both parties Chat filter blocks/flags messages matching configured patterns Live spectate shows real-time player actions for active investigations All moderation actions appear in action-audit with GM identity Bulk action processes 100+ players without timeout

features.title: Server-Authoritative Achievement System
features.description: Server-side achievement state management replacing localStorage, with tamper-resistant unlock validation, event-driven progress tracking from action-audit, cross-device sync, and anti-fraud enforcement where the client is display-only.

prd.purpose: Purpose Migrate achievements from client-side localStorage to a server-authoritative system where all unlock conditions are validated server-side, progress is tracked via action-audit events, and the client is display-only. Prevents achievement tampering and enables cross-device sync, leaderboards, and achievement-gated content.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements Achievement model with definition (name, description, icon, category, rarity), unlock conditions (event type + threshold), and progress tracking Server validates all unlock attempts — client never directly unlocks achievements Event ingestion pipeline: action-audit events (kills, trades, exploration, colonies, crafting, missions) are matched against achievement conditions Progress tracking: incremental achievements (e.g., "kill 100 pirates") maintain server-side counters Cross-device sync: achievement state fetched on login, cached client-side, invalidated on new unlocks via WebSocket Achievement categories: Combat, Trading, Exploration, Colonization, Social, Crafting, Missions, Special Rarity tiers: Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, Legendary — based on global unlock percentage Achievement-gated content: certain features/cosmetics require specific achievements Migration path: one-time import of existing localStorage achievements with server-side re-validation Anti-fraud: idempotent unlock processing, duplicate event filtering, rate limiting on progress updates
prd.data_model: Data Model Model/Column Type Notes Achievement New Model key UNIQUE, name, description, icon, category ENUM, rarity ENUM, unlock_condition JSONB, points INT AchievementProgress New Model user_id FK, achievement_id FK, current_count INT, target_count INT, updated_at AchievementUnlock New Model user_id FK, achievement_id FK, unlocked_at, source_event_id FK (action-audit ref), verified BOOLEAN
prd.api_surface: API Surface GET /api/achievements — list all achievement definitions GET /api/achievements/mine — player's progress and unlocks GET /api/achievements/player/:id — public achievement profile POST /api/achievements/migrate — one-time localStorage import with server re-validation Internal: AchievementService.processEvent(userId, eventType, eventData) — called by action-audit pipeline WebSocket: achievement_unlocked event pushed on new unlock
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Achievement notification toast on unlock (reuse existing notification system) Achievement page: grid/list view with progress bars, filter by category, sort by rarity Player profile: achievement showcase (pin up to 5 favorites) Achievement comparison between players
prd.dependencies: Dependencies action-audit (event source for all achievement triggers) achievements-client (this provides the backend; client becomes display-only) socket (real-time unlock notifications) anti-cheat-system (idempotency and fraud prevention)
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Event processing lag causes delayed unlocks. Mitigation: Direct event processing for common achievements; async batch processing for complex multi-condition achievements. Risk: localStorage migration imports fraudulent achievements. Mitigation: Server re-validates all imported achievements against action-audit history; unverifiable imports marked as "legacy" tier. Risk: Achievement condition changes invalidate existing unlocks. Mitigation: Unlocks are permanent once granted; condition changes only affect future unlocks; versioned achievement definitions.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Complex — 2 weeks. Achievement model, event processing pipeline, migration path, and anti-fraud layer.
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria All achievement unlocks validated server-side — client requests are rejected Action-audit events trigger achievement progress updates within 5 seconds Cross-device: unlock on device A visible on device B after re-login localStorage migration imports and re-validates existing achievements Achievement progress persists across sessions without client storage Duplicate events do not double-count progress Achievement rarity dynamically calculated from global unlock percentages

features.title: Player Reporting & Appeal System
features.description: Player-facing reporting and appeals interface: in-game report buttons on profiles and death screens, categorized reports with evidence attachment, appeal submission from account settings, resolution notifications, and GM priority queue integration.

prd.purpose: Purpose Provide the player-facing half of the moderation system: intuitive reporting UI, structured evidence collection, appeal submission, and transparent resolution communication. Works in tandem with moderation-tools (GM-facing) to create a complete moderation loop.
prd.functional_requirements: Functional Requirements In-game report button on player profiles (flag icon) and post-death/combat-loss screen Report categories: griefing, botting, exploiting, harassment, RMT (real-money trading), other Evidence attachment: automatic combat log link for combat-related reports; chat log references for harassment; free-text description field Report submission confirmation with tracking ID Appeal submission from account settings page when player has active penalty Appeal form: select penalty, write appeal text, attach supporting evidence Resolution notifications: in-app notification when report is resolved or appeal decided Report history: player can view their submitted reports and status (open/investigating/resolved/dismissed) Credibility scoring: reporters with high accuracy get reports prioritized; false reporters get deprioritized Rate limiting: max 5 reports per hour per player to prevent abuse

prd.api_surface: API Surface POST /api/reports — submit report with category and evidence GET /api/reports/mine — player's report history with status POST /api/appeals — submit appeal for active penalty GET /api/appeals/mine — player's appeal history GET /api/penalties/mine — player's active and historical penalties
prd.ux_specification: UX Specification Report button: small flag icon on player profile cards and combat result screen; opens modal with category selector and evidence form Report confirmation: toast with tracking ID and estimated review time Appeal form: accessible from account settings → "Penalties" tab; shows penalty details and appeal form Notification: bell icon badge for report/appeal resolution; notification details page Report history: table view with status badges (open=yellow, investigating=blue, resolved=green, dismissed=gray)
prd.dependencies: Dependencies moderation-tools (GM-facing counterpart; shared data models) notifications (resolution notifications) messaging (chat log evidence extraction) anti-cheat-system (report credibility feeds into anti-cheat signals)
prd.risks_mitigations: Risks & Mitigations Risk: Report spam overwhelming GM queue. Mitigation: Rate limiting (5/hour), credibility scoring, auto-deprioritize low-credibility reporters. Risk: Weaponized reporting (false reports to harass players). Mitigation: False report tracking; penalty for chronic false reporters; GM sees reporter history. Risk: Appeal backlog creates player frustration. Mitigation: SLA tracking on appeal response times; escalation to senior GM after 48h; transparent status updates.
prd.complexity_estimate: Complexity & Estimate Moderate — 1.5 weeks. Player-facing UI and API; data models shared with moderation-tools.
prd.acceptance_criteria: Acceptance Criteria Report button visible on all player profiles and post-combat screens Reports include category, description, and auto-attached combat/chat logs Player receives tracking ID on report submission Appeal form accessible for players with active penalties Resolution notifications delivered within 1 minute of GM action Report history shows accurate status for all submitted reports Rate limiting prevents more than 5 reports per hour per player
